title: crataegus oxyacantha var. praecox
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha var. praecox, also known as Early Hawthorn, is a variety of the hawthorn tree, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiotonic effects, which are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support heart health and overall well-being. Early Hawthorn is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various conditions, including digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and fever.
